summary:
The child may exhibit behavior associated with Oppositional Defiant Disorder due to lack of discipline at home; this includes defying authority figures like teachers as seen when mothers are overly permissive leading to potential aggression issues later manifesting through outbursts typical for IED/ODD diagnoses.
The primary concern here revolves around behavioral challenges stemming directly from insufficient guidance during formative years caused by excessively accommodating parents resulting primarily in non-compliance followed occasionally by intense emotional reactions characteristic across these disorders mentioned above. To summarize concisely while focusing only on critical elements,

A) Noncompliant & argumentative
B) Aggressively display emotions A) indicates likely resistance against directions whereas 
B) highlights more severe reactive tendencies possibly tied back causally via same root issue – inadequate limits set early life impacting social interactions negatively overtime especially regarding impulse control problems noted clinically within said conditions outlined initially. Thus final answer remains aligned choosing based upon most prominent observable trait after careful analysis provided options reflecting core diagnostic criteria discussed earlier. Agent1 concludes with selecting Option **\[**B]****, emphasizing its alignment better capturing overall essence highlighted throughout detailed examination conducted previously addressing underlying
